events.title: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
events.description: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destination A Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people. The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injured. Five of the dead were from mainland China and two from Hong Kong. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing

events.title: ‘Fuel on the fire’: Scientists confirm El Nino’s return, predict extreme weather effects
events.description: Meteorologists confirmed the arrival of El Nino in the Pacific Ocean on Thursday, warning that the climate pattern could grow into one of the strongest on record. Experts say it is likely to amplify global heat and fuel extreme weather including floods, droughts, wildfires and powerful storms in coming months.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Fuel on the fire’: Scientists confirm El Nino’s return, predict extreme weather effects

events.title: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
events.description: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout

events.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe
events.description: Violence spread across Belfast following a shocking knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese asylum seeker on Monday, triggering a wave of anti-immigration unrest. The victim suffered significant injuries to his face and back.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe

events.title: Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief
events.description: Nearly four months after the latest Middle East crisis erupted and despite a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, tensions continue to reverberate across the region and beyond. The UN Security Council held a high-level debate on advancing political solutions in the Middle East on Wednesday, amid continuing conflicts, humanitarian emergencies and concerns over regional stability. Follow live in-depth meetings coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief

events.title: Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing
events.description: Twenty people died and 120 others were injured in the explosion at the Erawan shrine 11 years ago.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai court sentences two men to death over Bangkok shrine bombing

events.title: Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths when powerful quake hit the Philippines
events.description: Philippine officials say that years of disaster-preparedness drills helped prevent a larger casualty toll when one of the strongest earthquakes in 50 years struck the south and left 55 people dead with 31 others still missing
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Disaster drills helped prevent more deaths when powerful quake hit the Philippines

events.title: Pokémon Go data trained AI that could assist military drones in war zones
events.description: Location scans from the globally popular augmented reality game have helped train AI to recognise and interpret physical spaces Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An AI model trained on data collected from users of Pokémon Go will potentially help military drones find their location in war zones. Pokémon Go, a 2016 augmented reality mobile game, allowed players to find and catch Pokémon in the real world using the cameras on their mobile phones, and exploded in popularity. In 2018, the company reported having more than 800m downloads worldwide. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pokémon Go data trained AI that could assist military drones in war zones

events.title: Amnesty and Oxfam warn of displacement in the occupied West Bank
events.description: Amnesty International and Oxfam released reports this week documenting a rise in state-backed Israeli settler violence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Amnesty and Oxfam warn of displacement in the occupied West Bank

events.title: Belfast unrest: 'Racists and groups allied to the far right are now unified across Europe'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Professor Peter Shirlow, Director of the Institute of Irish Studies at the University of Liverpool. Northern Ireland is once again confronting scenes of unrest, violence, and anti-migrant attacks. Shirlow frames the violence within a wider European and global context. He argues that today's far-right mobilisations are no longer isolated local phenomena but are increasingly interconnected through digital networks, transnational misinformation campaigns, and social media platforms that facilitate the rapid circulation of extremist narratives.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Belfast unrest: 'Racists and groups allied to the far right are now unified across Europe'

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: World News in Brief: Call for action against child labour, ICC Prosecutor suspended, WFP raises awareness in Egypt
events.description: Ahead of World Day Against Child Labour on 12 June, the UN is urging governments and communities to accelerate efforts to end a crisis that still affects millions of children worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Call for action against child labour, ICC Prosecutor suspended, WFP raises awareness in Egypt

events.title: Mountain of war: The India-Pakistan conflict’s deadliest battle zone
events.description: After their war last May, India and Pakistan reached a truce. But deaths continued on the world's highest battlefield.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Mountain of war: The India-Pakistan conflict’s deadliest battle zone

events.title: Vance Boelter changes plea to guilty in Minn. lawmaker shootings that killed Rep. Melissa Hortman, husband

events.title: Suspected Hamas terrorist arrested in Greece for allegedly plotting attack on Israeli cruise ship
events.description: A suspected Hamas terrorist reportedly granted asylum in Greece was arrested in Crete for allegedly plotting an attack on an Israeli cruise ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Suspected Hamas terrorist arrested in Greece for allegedly plotting attack on Israeli cruise ship

events.title: Christian leaders hold emergency summit in Jerusalem to confront global rise in antisemitism
events.description: International Christian Embassy Jerusalem is hosting an emergency summit addressing rising antisemitism and replacement theology amid church leaders' silence.

events.title: World court prosecutor who went after Netanyahu for war crimes suspended over sexual misconduct
events.description: Karim Khan, who sought arrest warrants for Netanyahu, suspended from the International Criminal Court amid allegations of serious misconduct involving nonconsensual sexual activity.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World court prosecutor who went after Netanyahu for war crimes suspended over sexual misconduct

events.title: ‘The future is peace’: An Israeli-Palestinian call for reconciliation
events.description: Aziz Abu Sarah is Palestinian. Maoz Inon is Israeli. Both their families have been bereaved by the endless conflict between their two communities. With their book "The Future is Peace" published in April, the two authors aim to rally international public opinion behind their project to put an end to the cycle of violence and find a path to reconciliation between Israelis and Palestinians. FRANCE 24 met them for an in-depth interview.

events.title: 'Acknowledging rights and needs of Israelis and Palestinians: Shared path towards a secure future'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Avi Meyerstein, co-founder and president of the Alliance for Middle East Peace (ALLMEP). At a moment when international attention is increasingly consumed by conflict across the Middle East, Meyer argues that the crises currently unfolding are not isolated events but manifestations of unresolved political conflicts that have been left to fester for decades. "While those things remain unresolved," he observes, any semblance of stability "is just an illusion."
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Acknowledging rights and needs of Israelis and Palestinians: Shared path towards a secure future'

events.title: Iran's football team trains in Mexico as World Cup campaign begins
events.description: Iran's national football team held its first open training session in Mexico on Thursday, offering a glimpse of a squad preparing for the World Cup under the shadow of conflict with the US as President Donald Trump claimed a breakthrough in efforts to end the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's football team trains in Mexico as World Cup campaign begins

events.title: British Muslim police group called IDF a terrorist organization, questioned Hamas atrocity reports
events.description: A U.K. Muslim police group's paper allegedly calls IDF a terrorist group and disputes Oct. 7 atrocities, prompting calls for investigation and dismissals.
events.ticker_label: 💥 British Muslim police group called IDF a terrorist organization, questioned Hamas atrocity reports

events.title: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
events.description: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North Sumatra Extreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival. The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans ( Pongo tapanuliensis ) were killed after more than 1,000mm (39in) of rain fell over four days in Indonesia’s North Sumatra province in November 2025. This equates to 11% of the local population and 7% of the entire species. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds

events.title: Police officer in Toronto killed in shooting linked to investigation of a US Consulate attack
events.description: Police in Toronto say an officer was shot and killed by a suspect in the investigation into a shooting attack that damaged the facade of the U.S. Consulate in the Canadian city in March
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Police officer in Toronto killed in shooting linked to investigation of a US Consulate attack

events.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
events.description: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say

events.title: ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims
events.description: Ministry says animals fitted with sensors by foreign agencies collect sensitive sea data, in ‘invisible secret war’ China’s ministry of state security has claimed that foreign espionage and intelligence agencies are using innovative new methods to monitor the country’s waters, including deploying “spy” animals fitted with sensors. In a post on the Chinese platform WeChat on Friday, the ministry warned that an “invisible secret war” was quietly playing out in the seas around China as foreign agencies were collecting sensitive data “through a variety of new spying devices” to produce underwater maps that pose a “serious threat to our national security”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims

events.title: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
events.description: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever

events.title: Weather tracker: heat, humidity and thunderstorms pose risk to World Cup
events.description: With matches in 16 cities across the US, Mexico and Canada, players and fans face an array of weather-related challenges With the 2026 World Cup now under way, all 48 teams face a common opposition: summer weather across North America. Matches will be played in 16 cities, from southern Mexico to Canada, with a range of weather risks possible at each venue. Thunderstorms have already disrupted play before the tournament has even begun. England’s warm-up against Costa Rica in Orlando was delayed by around an hour after storms brought lightning and heavy rain that waterlogged the pitch. Safety regulations at US venues mean play is suspended when lightening is recorded within roughly eight miles of a stadium, not resuming until 30 minutes after the last strike. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: heat, humidity and thunderstorms pose risk to World Cup

events.title: Racist riots break out across United Kingdom
events.description: A video of a knife attack sparked racist riots across the Northern Irish capital Belfast on Monday, with violence has now spreading across the United Kingdom. Keir Starmer says there will be "no tolerance" for rioters.

events.title: As the world heats up, cities work to cool down
events.description: Global warming trends mean more frequent, intense heat waves. Cities around the world are working to mitigate extreme heat and adapt urban life to climate change.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As the world heats up, cities work to cool down

events.title: Jemma Stapleton, Australian sprinter and Stawell Gift finalist, dies aged 25 on family holiday
events.description: Flood of tributes for Victorian athlete, who died while overseas Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Local Australian athletics organisations have paid tribute to 2025 Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton, 25, who died while on holiday overseas with her family. The cause of death has not yet been disclosed, though an online fundraising page shared by her brother said she “tragically lost her life in an accident”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Jemma Stapleton, Australian sprinter and Stawell Gift finalist, dies aged 25 on family holiday

events.title: A fire on the Spanish island of Mallorca kills 2 people and injures several

events.title: ‘Human rights are part of our DNA’: UN launches global alliance to counter rising threats
events.description: The UN human rights office (OHCHR) has launched a Global Alliance for Human Rights, a broad coalition aimed at placing the issue at the heart of decision-making, when conflict levels have reached a record high amid deepening inequality and accelerating climate change.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening
events.description: The Security Council met on Monday amid a sharp escalation in hostilities across Ukraine, where UN officials warned that the war has reached its deadliest point since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022. Briefing members, Rosemary DiCarlo, Under-Secretary-General for political affairs, said recent months had seen some of the most extensive aerial attacks of the conflict, while the humanitarian toll on civilians continued to mount on both sides of the front line.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening

events.title: Hate speech spreading ‘faster than ever’, warns UN chief as global action plan is revealed

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hate speech spreading ‘faster than ever’, warns UN chief as global action plan is revealed

events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds

events.title: Trump says Iran peace deal is near after threatening new strikes
events.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday he had halted plans for new military strikes on Iran, claiming negotiators were close to extending a fragile ceasefire. Hours before, he threatened to intensify the conflict by targeting Iran’s oil industry.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says Iran peace deal is near after threatening new strikes

events.title: UN rights chief urges ‘massive rethink’ of US immigration policies ahead of World Cup

events.title: Cuba hopes for World Cup respite from US sabre-rattling – but prepares for the worst
events.description: With some matches being held in nearby Miami, a Cuban response to US military action could mar the tournament As Cuba crumbles under a nearly five-month-long US oil blockade, many on the island hope that the World Cup might save the island from US attack – or at least offer a respite until the competition ends on 19 July. “The beginning of the World Cup will make it more difficult for the United States to carry out a military action in Cuba,” said Carlos Alzugaray, Cuba’s former ambassador to the EU. “Cuba is very close to the US, and can hit many targets inside the US, especially in south Florida, with drones or other weapons.” Continue reading...

events.title: Middle East live: US forces shoot down Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: US forces shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones as Tehran appeared to attempt to strike commercial ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, hours after US President Donald Trump announced a "great settlement" to end the war. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

events.title: Risk of 'very strong' El Nino raising global temperatures, scientists warn

events.title: Japan reactor restart sparks fresh fears over nuclear waste storage
events.description: The reboot highlights a dire problem for the country's nuclear program. Japan is running out of space to store spent nuclear fuel and lacks plans for radioactive waste disposal.

events.title: Number of people forced to flee their homes has dropped, U.N. report finds
events.description: A U.N. report finds the number of people uprooted by conflicts around the world has fallen and the number of refugees returning home is reaching record numbers. But some haven't returned by choice.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Mexico beat South Africa in opening match
events.description: The 2026 World Cup began in Mexico City with an opening ceremony filled with colour, dancing and a performance by global music star Shakira. Julian Quinones and Premier League player Raul Jimenez scored the first goals of the tournament.

events.title: Refugee numbers drop for first time in a decade, but millions remain trapped
events.description: Global forced displacement has decreased for the first time in a decade, the UN refugee agency (UNHCR) reported on Thursday, though the figure remains unacceptably high and tens of millions of people are still trapped in prolonged exile with little prospect of rebuilding their lives.

events.title: Toronto police officer shot while investigating US consulate attack
events.description: Suspect, 19, still at large after officer dies in hospital having been shot while searching an apartment A Toronto police officer has been shot dead as police raided an apartment allegedly linked to the March attack on the US consulate, the city’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, said. Demkiw said 43-year-old constable Marc Pinizzotto was shot while conducting an early-morning search warrant in the north-west of the city and later died at a hospital. Continue reading...

events.description: Officials say two people were killed in Russia's Bryansk, while a woman was killed in Ukraine's Sumy.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Central Africa in the spotlight, including outlawed Lord's Resistance Army
events.description: The Security Council meets this morning to discuss the situation in Central Africa, including efforts to combat the so-called Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), one of the region’s longest-running security threats, responsible for over 100,000 deaths, the UN estimates. Ambassadors have been briefed by the regional office (UNOCA) and its strategy for improving civilian protection, humanitarian access and cross-border cooperation.

events.title: Inside Tehran: Iranians describe IRGC's brutal rule, poverty — ask Trump to 'stay the course'

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside

events.title: What's in the landmark EU migration reform hardening border protections?
events.description: A major EU immigration reform allowing member states to reduce protections for asylum-seekers comes into force on Friday. The changes have prompted right groups to warn that humanitarian concerns are taking a backseat to politics in Europe, where public opinion has hardened on migration.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What's in the landmark EU migration reform hardening border protections?

events.title: South Korea sentences ex-president Yoon to 30 years over North Korea drone incident
events.description: South Korea on Friday sentenced ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years in prison for sending military drones into North Korea in 2024 in a bid to "heighten inter-Korean military tensions and manufacture a national crisis" enabling him to declare martial law. Yeol was in February given a life imprisonment sentence – which he is appealing – for leading an insurrection with his martial law declaration.

events.title: DR Congo's World Cup team cleared for US entry after Ebola quarantine
events.description: DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre said Thursday he hopes his side can deliver a strong World Cup showing after arriving in the US following an imposed quarantine period linked to the Ebola outbreak. The team, making their first appearance at the tournament since 1974, have relocated preparations abroad but aim to bring some emotional relief to supporters back home.

events.title: ‘Rare, untreatable strain’: Ebola toll mounts in eastern DR Congo
events.description: The top UN aid official in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is in Ituri province – the epicentre of the country's Ebola outbreak – for a three-day assessment visit, as the confirmed case count reaches 515 across three eastern provinces.

events.title: Missing boy Gus Lamont’s grandmother fined for firearms offence unrelated to his disappearance
events.description: Josie Murray fined $10,500 and disqualified from gun ownership for five years after pleading guilty to possessing a gun silencer Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The grandmother of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont has been fined $10,500 after admitting to a firearms offence unrelated to the boy’s disappearance eight months ago. Josie Murray was fined $10,500 after a hearing in Adelaide magistrates court on Friday, where she pleaded guilty to one aggravated charge of possessing a sound moderator – a device better known as a gun silencer. Continue reading...

events.title: Funeral is held for 22 Pakistani soldiers who died in a helicopter crash in Kashmir

events.title: Canadian mother sues OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT led her daughter to kill herself
events.description: Suit filed in US alleges chatbot told Alice Carrier, 24, ‘maybe this is just the end’ as she struggled with suicidal thoughts A Canadian mother sued OpenAI and its CEO, Sam Altman, in US court on Thursday, alleging that ChatGPT encouraged her daughter to kill herself. The lawsuit is the latest in a slew accusing the company of failing to address dangerous conversations between users and the company’s chatbot. Kristie Carrier said in a lawsuit filed in San Francisco state court that her daughter, Alice, told ChatGPT about her suicidal ideations more than a dozen times leading up to her death but that OpenAI’s safety systems never flagged the conversations for human review or terminated them. Continue reading...

events.title: One Nation fundraiser moved from Melbourne restaurant amid planned protests
events.description: Event to go ahead at an undisclosed location after Moonee Ponds restaurant cancelled at the eleventh hour Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation has been forced to move a planned fundraising event to an undisclosed location in Melbourne, after the venue decided not to hold it due to planned protest activity. The event was due to be attended by One Nation leader Pauline Hanson and Barnaby Joyce. A spokesperson for the party told Guardian Australia on Friday that it would move the fundraiser from Giorgio Casa, a bistro in Moonee Ponds, after Victorian police “did express concern about protest activity at the event”. events.title: Bandits in north-west Nigeria abduct villagers they invited to discuss peace talks
events.description: Thirty-nine people taken near Magamin Diddi village in Maradun municipality, north-west Zamfara state, police say Armed bandits in north-west Nigeria abducted dozens of villagers whom they invited to a meeting about potential peace negotiations, authorities and residents said on Monday, highlighting the region’s worsening security. According to local police, 39 people were seized on Sunday during a meeting in the forest near Magamin Diddi village in the Maradun municipality of north-west Zamfara state. But some residents and officials believe the number of those abducted could be as high as 50. Continue reading...

events.title: 'What makes Gaudí and Sagrada Família so universal is precisely it being so local, so Catalan'
events.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Antoni Vives, former Deputy Mayor of Barcelona in charge of urban planning. According to Vives, the Sagrada Família is not simply an architectural masterpiece. It is a living demonstration of how authenticity, rootedness, and transcendence can converge in a single human project. Its significance lies not only in its extraordinary design, but in its ability to connect the local with the universal, the contemporary with the eternal, and nature with all that's sacred.

events.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term for drone flights over Pyongyang
events.description: South Korea's ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister were sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang in 2024 to heighten tensions with North Korea and justify declaring martial law at home.

events.title: The Teddy Bear Doctor treats stuffies in need of care
events.description: Ruth Hasman's home in British Columbia is known as the teddy bear hospital -- a place where stuffies in need of repair receive the TLC they need.

events.title: Taiwan opposition leader says Xi meeting avoided 'reunification' talk
events.description: Cheng's comments come as Taiwan awaits approval on a $14 billion arms package from the U.S., and as uncertainty lingers over Washington's long-term commitment to the island's defense.

events.title: How 'algorithm' got its name from a 9th-century Persian mathematician
events.description: In the 9th century, Muhammad ibn Musa al-Kharizmi helped solidify the concept of algorithms in mathematics and popularized algebra and the use of the zero.

events.title: South Korea: Ex-President Yoon gets 30 years for sending drones into North Korea
events.description: The verdict marks the latest legal blow for South Korea's impeached former leader Yoon Suk Yeol, who has already been sentenced to life in prison earlier this year.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.description: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: CEAS comes into effect: How will it impact asylum seekers?
events.description: The German interior minister aims to further reduce the number of new asylum seekers. The reformed EU asylum law (CEAS) comes into effect Friday, but experts say it is unlikely to achieve all it sets out to do.

events.title: UK: Defense Secretary John Healey quits in military spending dispute
events.description: Britain's defense minister has abruptly quit his post, saying the government's plans to bolster defense spending in the coming years are inadequate. The move puts additional pressure on embattled UK PM Keir Starmer.

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.description: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Funeral for former French first lady Bernadette Chirac to take place in Paris
events.description: The funeral of Bernadette Chirac, widow of former president Jacques Chirac, will take place on Friday at the Basilica of Sainte-Clotilde in Paris. Chirac died on June 5 at the age of 93.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Funeral for former French first lady Bernadette Chirac to take place in Paris

events.title: Thai princess dies aged 47
events.description: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha has died after nearly four years in a coma, aged 47.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai princess dies aged 47

events.title: Five claims from former Palestinian prime minister - and why they matter
events.description: We spent an hour talking to former Palestinian prime minister Mohammad Shtayyeh, who is one of the most influential voices in the West Bank.

events.title: 'I'm no cold-blooded professional hitman', says teenager accused of UK assassination plot
events.description: A Norwegian teenager accused of flying to the UK to assassinate a stranger for money has told a court he's "no cold-blooded professional hitman".

events.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term for drone flights over Pyongyang
events.description: South Korea’s ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol and his former defense minister have been sentenced to 30 years in prison in a case alleging Yoon ordered drone flights over Pyongyang to heighten tensions with North Korea and and justify declaring martial ...

events.title: 'King of the North' seeks path to becoming Britain's next leader in special election
events.description: About 75,000 voters in northwest England are about to make a significant decision

events.title: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build a new US Consulate
events.description: Foreign workers building a sprawling $350 million American Consulate in Milan say they were paid less than $2 an hour after being promised fair wages

events.title: A Thai woman faces murder charges in Myanmar in the killing of a US diplomat
events.description: A Thai woman has appeared in a Myanmar court on murder charges in the killing of an American diplomat found dead about two weeks ago at a Yangon hotel

events.title: ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine
events.description: As Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine enters its fifth year, conditions in southern Ukraine have rapidly deteriorated. Maintaining humanitarian operations has become an increasingly complex balancing act between enabling aid delivery and managing risk.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine

events.title: Pima County sheriff says Mexican authorities have not made contact over Nancy Guthrie search tip
events.description: Pima County Sheriff says department has not been contacted by Mexican authorities after anonymous tip suggests Nancy Guthrie's grave was found.

events.title: Man in viral Karmelo Anthony courthouse confrontation arrested at murder trial
events.description: At least two arrests were made outside a Texas courthouse after Karmelo Anthony was found guilty of murder and sentenced to 35 years behind bars.

events.title: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony claims he is 'penniless' in appeal despite major donor-funded legal campaign
events.description: Karmelo Anthony, convicted of stabbing Austin Metcalf at a Texas track meet, claims he is indigent and requests a free public defender for his appeal.

events.title: Austin Metcalf's dad expresses empathy for Karmelo Anthony as killer's parents say 'they did a number on us'
events.description: Karmelo Anthony's family claims injustice after murder conviction, while victim Austin Metcalf's father expresses sorrow for the convicted killer.

events.title: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony's family used fundraiser money for moving and living expenses
events.description: Karmelo Anthony's family fundraiser raised nearly $634,000 before being taken down after his murder conviction and 35-year prison sentence.

events.title: Minnesota man marks FBI's first arrest from DOJ's 'Most Wanted Fraudsters' list
events.description: FBI Director Kash Patel announced the first arrest from the DOJ's Most Wanted Fraudsters list as a Minneapolis grocery owner faces fraud charges.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.

events.title: Colombian lawmakers seek suspension of Trump foe Gustavo Petro over alleged meddling in upcoming election
events.description: Colombian lawmakers consider suspending President Gustavo Petro from office amid allegations he improperly intervened in the presidential election.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Serbian President Vučić says support for US 'surged' under Trump, invites him to visit Belgrade
events.description: Serbian President Vučić says relations with the U.S. have transformed under Trump, citing economic cooperation and an invitation for Trump to visit.

events.title: Finland’s foreign minister says Ukraine ‘is now holding the cards’ as Russia signals talks
events.description: Finnish Foreign Minister Elina Valtonen says Ukraine is holding the cards today as Russia renews talk of negotiations amid Kyiv's military gains.
